claimant_lives_in_uk.description: Whether the claimant lives in the UK.

children.description: A list of children the claimant is claiming for.

lives_with_claimant.description: Whether the child lives with the claimant.

in_approved_education.description: Whether the child is in approved education or training (e.g. A-levels, GCSEs, NVQ up to level 3). Only relevant for children aged 16-19.

in_extension_period.description: Whether the child (aged 16 or 17) has left education and registered with a government-sponsored careers service or the armed forces (qualifying for a 20-week extension).

upkeep_per_week.description: The amount in £ per week the claimant contributes towards the child's upkeep (food, clothes, pocket money, etc.). Only relevant if the child does not live with the claimant.

another_claimant_has_priority.description: Whether another person who also lives with the child has priority for claiming Child Benefit.

another_claimant_lives_with_child.description: Whether someone who lives with the child is already claiming Child Benefit for them.

care_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in local authority care.

care_home_24h_per_week.description: Whether the child spends at least 24 hours per week at home while in local authority care.

hospital_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in hospital or residential accommodation.

claimant_spends_on_child.description: Whether the claimant regularly spends money on the child (e.g. food, clothes, books) while the child is in hospital or residential accommodation.

is_fostered.description: Whether the child is fostered by the claimant.

council_pays_for_child.description: Whether the local council pays towards the child's accommodation or maintenance.

works_24_plus_hours.description: Whether the child works 24 or more hours per week in paid employment.

started_apprenticeship_in_england.description: Whether the child has started an apprenticeship in England.

receives_qualifying_benefits.description: Whether the child receives qualifying benefits in their own right, such as Universal Credit or Employment and Support Allowance.

test_case.agent_script:
=== YOUR SITUATION PROFILE ===
You do not live in the UK.
You are inquiring about your 2 children:
  - Alex is 18 years old and lives with you.
  - Blake is 13 years old and lives with you.

Here are the exact details of your circumstances to use when answering the agent's questions:

Regarding Alex:
  - Claimant does not live in the UK
  - Child is 18 and not in approved education or extension period
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child in care for 7 weeks (within 8-week limit)
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

Regarding Blake:
  - Claimant does not live in the UK
  - Child is 13 (under 16)
  - Another claimant who lives with the child has priority
  - Child in care for 16 weeks but spends 24+ hours/week at home
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

expected_eligibility.name: Alex
expected_eligibility.reason: Claimant does not live in the UK; Child is 18 and not in approved education or extension period

Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: I'd be happy to help you determine if you're eligible for UK Child Benefit. Let me start the assessment for you.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Great! Let's begin the Child Benefit eligibility assessment.

**First, I need to know about the child (or children) you're claiming for.**

**How old is the child?**

Please let me know which category applies:
- **Under 16 years old**
- **16 to 19 years old**
- **20 years old or over**

If you're claiming for multiple children, please tell me about each of them and their ages.

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text:
Oh, hello! Yes, I'm interested in claiming Child Benefit and wanted to check if I'm eligible.

I have two children actually - Alex is 18 and Blake is 13. They both live with me at home.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Hello! It's great that you're checking your eligibility. I'd be happy to help you work through this for both Alex (18) and Blake (13).

Since your children are in different age categories, let me assess them one at a time.

---

**Let's start with Blake (13 years old):**

Since Blake is under 16, they automatically meet the age requirement for Child Benefit. Now I need to check a few more things.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
**For Blake (13):** Is Blake working less than 24 hours per week, or not in paid work at all?

*(This is usually straightforward for younger children who are typically in school full-time, but I need to confirm.)*

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, Blake doesn't work at all - he's just in school like any normal 13-year-old!

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Perfect, that's what I expected! Blake is in school and not working, so that's a yes - Blake is working less than 24 hours per week.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Great! Next question for Blake:

**Is Blake NOT receiving certain benefits in his own right?**

Specifically, is Blake **not** claiming any of the following benefits himself:
- Employment and Support Allowance (ESA)
- Universal Credit

*(This would be unusual for a 13-year-old, but I need to check.)*

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, Blake doesn't receive any benefits in his own name. He's just a regular kid!

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: That's what I thought! Let me continue checking the requirements for Blake.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
**Is Blake in an apprenticeship?**

Since Blake is 13 and in school, I assume he's **not** in an apprenticeship - is that correct?

*(Just to explain: certain types of apprenticeships can affect Child Benefit eligibility, but this typically applies to older children.)*

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: That's right, he's definitely not doing an apprenticeship - he's just 13 and in regular school!

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Exactly as expected! Blake is not in an apprenticeship, so let's continue.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Now I need to check your residency status. 

**Do you meet UK residency requirements?**

You qualify if **any one** of the following applies to you:

1. **You normally live in the UK**
2. You have **settled status** under the EU Settlement Scheme
3. You have **pre-settled status** and are working (earning above the National Insurance threshold)
4. You have **pre-settled status** and are actively seeking work
5. You have **pre-settled status** with sufficient resources to support yourself
6. You have **pre-settled status** and are a student with sufficient resources
7. You have **pre-settled status** as a family member of an EEA/Swiss national
8. You're a **Crown servant** living abroad

Do any of these apply to you? (Most commonly, people simply "normally live in the UK")

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, I don't actually live in the UK. I'm living abroad at the moment.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for letting me know. Since you're living abroad, I need to check if you might still qualify through one of the special circumstances.

**Are you a Crown servant living abroad?**

This would include:
- Serving in HM Armed Forces overseas
- Working for the UK Government abroad (e.g., diplomats, civil servants posted overseas)
- Working in certain other qualifying roles abroad for the UK

Does this apply to your situation?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, I'm not a Crown servant or working for the UK government. I'm just living abroad.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for clarifying. Unfortunately, based on what you've told me, you don't meet the UK residency requirements for Child Benefit.

Since you:
- Don't normally live in the UK
- Don't have settled or pre-settled status under the EU Settlement Scheme
- Are not a Crown servant living abroad

This means you wouldn't be eligible for Child Benefit for either Blake or Alex at this time.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
---

## Assessment Result: NOT ELIGIBLE

I'm sorry, but based on the information you've provided, **you are not currently eligible for Child Benefit for either Alex or Blake**.

### Summary:

| Child | Age | Eligible? | Reason |
|-------|-----|-----------|--------|
| **Alex** | 18 | ❌ No | Claimant doesn't meet residency requirements |
| **Blake** | 13 | ❌ No | Claimant doesn't meet residency requirements |

### Why?

UK Child Benefit has specific residency requirements for the **claimant** (you). To be eligible, you must:
- Normally live in the UK, **OR**
- Have settled or pre-settled status under the EU Settlement Scheme (with qualifying criteria), **OR**
- Be a Crown servant living abroad

Since you're living abroad and don't fall into any of these categories, you cannot claim Child Benefit at this time — even though Blake would otherwise meet all the child-related criteria.

### What You Can Do:

- **If your circumstances change** and you return to live in the UK, you may become eligible at that point
- **If you work for the UK government abroad** in the future, you may qualify as a Crown servant
- You may wish to check if you're entitled to any family benefits in the country where you currently reside

I'm sorry I couldn't bring better news. Is there anything else you'd like me to clarify?

reasonings: Alex (18 years old): The claimant does not meet UK residency requirements. To claim Child Benefit, the claimant must normally live in the UK, have settled or pre-settled status under the EU Settlement Scheme (with qualifying criteria), or be a Crown servant living abroad. Since none of these apply, the claimant is not eligible for Child Benefit for Alex.

Blake (13 years old): Although Blake meets all the child-related criteria (under 16, not working, not receiving benefits, not in an apprenticeship), the claimant does not meet UK residency requirements. To claim Child Benefit, the claimant must normally live in the UK, have settled or pre-settled status under the EU Settlement Scheme (with qualifying criteria), or be a Crown servant living abroad. Since none of these apply, the claimant is not eligible for Child Benefit for Blake.
arguments.overall_reasoning: The claimant is living abroad and is not a Crown servant or working for the UK government. They do not have settled or pre-settled status under the EU Settlement Scheme. UK Child Benefit requires the claimant to meet specific residency requirements, which this claimant does not satisfy. Therefore, they are not eligible to claim Child Benefit for either of their children, regardless of whether the children themselves would otherwise qualify.

response.description: Determine child's age category for eligibility
response.routing_logic: Under 16: automatic qualification. 16-19: check education/training. 20+: not eligible

response.question: Is the child working less than 24 hours per week (or not in paid work)?
response.help_text: Children aged 16+ working 24+ hours per week and not in approved education/training are not eligible

response.question: Is the child NOT receiving certain benefits in their own right (e.g., ESA, Universal Credit)?
response.help_text: Children receiving Employment and Support Allowance or Universal Credit in their own right are not eligible

response.question: Is the child NOT in a disqualifying apprenticeship?
response.help_text: Regional rules apply: English apprenticeships (all types) disqualify. Scottish Modern Apprenticeships disqualify. Welsh Foundation Apprenticeships are APPROVED. Other Welsh apprenticeships disqualify.

response.question: Does the claimant meet UK residency requirements?
response.help_text: Must normally live in the UK or meet special circumstances

paths.requirement: Claimant normally lives in the UK

paths.description: Has settled status under EU Settlement Scheme
paths.requirement: Has settled status under EU Settlement Scheme

criteria: Has pre-settled status under EU Settlement Scheme
Working and earning/expect to earn above primary National Insurance threshold for 3 continuous months

paths.description: Pre-settled status and actively seeking work
criteria: Has pre-settled status under EU Settlement Scheme
Looking for work (can claim as jobseeker for up to 91 days)
Has pre-settled status under EU Settlement Scheme
Has sufficient resources to support self and family financially
NOT claiming Income Support, income-based JSA, Pension Credit, or Universal Credit
Has pre-settled status under EU Settlement Scheme
Studying and has sufficient resources to support self financially

paths.description: Pre-settled status as family member of EEA/Swiss national
criteria: Has pre-settled status under EU Settlement Scheme
Is family member of EEA or Swiss national with right to reside
One of: spouse/civil partner, dependent child/grandchild under 21, dependent parent/grandparent

paths.requirement: Living abroad as Crown servant or in certain qualifying countries

response.evaluation_logic: If ANY path is satisfied, proceed to responsibility check

response.reason: Does not meet UK residency requirements
response.guidance: Must normally live in the UK or meet special circumstances (settled status, pre-settled status with qualifying criteria, Crown servant, or living in certain countries). If you have pre-settled status, you may need to be working, studying, or have sufficient resources.
